claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with the basics:

**How many children would you like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's lovely to meet you, and I'm happy to help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask a few more questions:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care and upbringing?** (For example, are you paying for things like food, clothes, and her general day-to-day needs?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of all her needs - food, clothes, school things, everything she needs day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for that.

**Is Alex in full-time education or training?** Since she's 6 years old, I assume she's in primary school, but I just want to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - she's in primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more question to confirm:

**Are you living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for all her day-to-day needs (food, clothes, school things, etc.)
- Alex is in full-time education (primary school)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Age requirement met** – Alex is 6 years old (under 16)
✅ **UK residence** – You live in the UK
✅ **Responsibility** – Alex lives with you and you are responsible for her care and upbringing
✅ **Education** – Alex is in full-time education (primary school)
✅ **No duplicate claims** – No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can apply for Child Benefit online through the official government website. If you have any other questions about Child Benefit or the application process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK, which is a core eligibility requirement. Alex lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for her care and upbringing (providing food, clothes, and all day-to-day needs). This satisfies the 'responsible for a child' requirement. Alex is in approved education (primary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for that child. The claimant should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for their daughter Alex.

This satisfies the 'responsible for a child' requirement. Alex is in approved education (primary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
